notes: Shah Jahan (Mirza Shahab-ud-din Baig Muhammad Khan Khurram). He was the fifth Mughal emperor and reigned from 1628 to 1658. Aurangzeb declared himself emperor in 1658 and imprisoned Shah Jahan in Agra Fort until his death in January 1666.
Dara Shikoh. The Battle of Deorai was fought between Aurangzeb and Dara Shikoh. It was fought in 1659. Dara Shikoh was defeated by Aurangzeb in this Battle.
Abdul Hamid Lahori - A historian and traveler who later became a court historian of Shah Jahan. “Badshah Nama” (Padshahnama) is the official history of Shah Jahan.

Ustad Ahmad Lahori was the chief Mughal architect during the reign of Shah Jahan. He was responsible for the construction of the Taj Mahal in Agra and the Red fort in Delhi. Q.410. The art of decoration, called Pietra dura became popular during whose reign? SSC CGL 19/07/2023 (4th shift) (a) Jahangir (b) Sher Shah Suri (c) Shah Jahan (d) Akbar Sol.410.(c) Shah Jahan. Pietra dura - An inlay technique using cut and fitted, highly polished colored stones to create images. Some other buildings and monuments by Shah Jahan:- Red Fort (Delhi), Jama Masjid (Delhi), Shish Mahal (Agra) and Shalimar Bagh (Lahore).
In 1632, during the period of Shah Jahan, Ahmednagar was merged into the Mughal empire. Malik Ahmed was the founder of Ahmednagar (Maharashtra), who established the Nizam Shahi dynasty.

Dara Shikoh (Eldest Son of Shah Jahan). Sultan Buland Iqbal (Prince of High Fortune) - Title given on 10 September 1642. His works - Majma -ul-Bahrain (Mingling of Two Oceans), Sirr-i-Akbar (Great Mystery). He was executed in 1659 by Aurangzeb in the war of succession. Aurangzeb Q.413. Which Mughal ruler was responsible for the execution of Guru Tegh Bahadur in the year 1675 ? SSC CHSL 11/07/2024 (1st shift) (a) Jahangir (b) Akbar (c) Aurangzeb (d) Shahjahan Sol.413.(c) Aurangzeb. Guru Tegh Bahadur was the ninth Sikh Guru, often venerated as the ‘Protector of Humanity’ (Srisht-di-Chadar) by the Sikhs. He founded Anandpur Sahib.
Aurangzeb (1658 - 1707) ascended the throne in after eliminating all competitors, including Dara Shukoh, Shuja, and Murad, in a war of succession.

Aurangzeb (1658-1707). His Military Campaigns : In the north-east, the Ahoms were defeated in 1663, but rebelled again in the 1680s. Campaigns in the north-west against the Yusufzai and the Sikhs were temporarily successful.

Aurangzeb (Reign : 1658-1707) - He was the sixth Mughal Emperor and also known as Zinda Pir (living saint), Fakir, and Darbesh. He gave the French permission to build a factory in Surat in 1667. Q.417. Under whose reign was the maximum number of books in Persian on classical Indian Music written? SSC CGL 20/07/2023 (2nd shift) (a) Shah Jahan (b) Jahangir (c) Akbar (d) Aurangzeb Sol.417.(d) Aurangzeb (1658 - 1707). He reimposed Jizya and Pilgrim tax in 1679 and ended the celebration of Navroz. Q.418. Which Mughal Emperor has attempted to vandalise the Kailash Temple at Ellora? SSC MTS 02/05/2023 (Evening) (a) Aurangzeb (b) Humayun (c) Shah Jahan (d) Babur Sol.418.(a) Aurangzeb {Alamgir, original name MuḥI al-Din Muḥammad (1658 to 1707)} - He stopped music and alcohol in the court and also the activities of ‘jharokha’ (public audience). Sikh Guru Q.419. Gurpurab is celebrated to commemorate the birthday of Guru Nanak dev ji. He was the _________ guru of Sikhs? SSC CHSL 10/07/2024 (3rd shift) (a) second (b) first (c) fourth (d) third Sol.419.(b) First. Guru Nanak Dev, the founder of Sikhism, was born on April 15, 1469, in Talwandi, now known as Nankana Sahib, Pakistan.
Mughals. Guru Gobind Singh is famous for his creation of the Khalsa History (Pure) , the military brotherhood of the Sikhs.

Guru Arjan Dev assisted Khusrau, the rebellious son of Jahangir. He compiled the first official edition of the Sikh scripture called the Adi Granth, which later expanded into the Guru Granth Sahib. He completed the construction of the Golden Temple in Amritsar.

Guru Gobind Singh laid the foundation of ‘Khalsa Panth’ on April 13, 1699, situated in Punjab. It is a name given to the religious structure and the community that manages the affairs of the global Khalsa community.
